events.title: US-Iran Military Escalation
events.description: Major US-Israeli military operations against Iran since March 2026, causing oil prices to spike above $110-117 per barrel and threatening Strait of Hormuz closure.

events.title: Federal Reserve Hawks Maintain Higher Rates
events.description: Fed holds rates at 3.50-3.75% despite geopolitical tensions, prioritizing inflation fight over traditional market support amid energy-driven price pressures.

events.title: US-China Trade War Escalation
events.description: Supreme Court struck down Trump's IEEPA tariffs but new 10% global tariff imposed; China faces 33.9% effective tariff rate while launching Section 301 investigations.

events.title: Germany-Europe Economic Crisis Deepens
events.description: Germany faces second consecutive year of recession with 0.2% contraction, while Europe operates near stagnation with heightened debt crisis sensitivity.

events.title: Russia-Ukraine Energy Ceasefire Collapse
events.description: Fragile US-brokered energy infrastructure ceasefire repeatedly violated, disrupting Black Sea grain exports and European energy supplies amid failed peace talks.

overall_summary: March 2026 presents a classic stagflationary environment with simultaneous supply shocks driving inflation while hawkish central bank policy constrains growth. Geopolitical tensions from Iran conflict and trade wars create persistent energy and commodity price pressures, while structural economic weakness in Europe and fragile ceasefires compound uncertainty.
